name: ui-migration
description: Migrate from old UI (app/) to new UI (ekoa/): rename old folder, update docs, review and fix new UI, test builder flow

members.name: ui-reviewer
members.prompt:
You are part of the ui-migration team. Your task is to review and fix the new UI builder flow in the ekoa/ folder by comparing it with the old UI in _app-reference/.

## Context
The old UI (now at _app-reference/) is being deprecated in favor of the new UI (ekoa/). The new UI's builder needs fixes to work properly.

## Critical Issues to Fix

### 1. Preview auto-start on template copy
In `ekoa/hooks/useAgentExecution.ts`, the preview currently only starts when the job completes (in the useEffect that watches `streamState.isComplete`). It should ALSO start as soon as template files are copied - right after `executeAgent()` returns and we process `jobInfo.templateFiles`.

Add a `startPreview()` call after the template files processing (around line 195) so the user sees the preview updating in real-time as the agent modifies files. Keep the existing completion preview restart too.

### 2. SSE streaming URL mismatch
Check if the new UI's streaming URL matches what the API expects:
- New UI (ekoa/hooks/useJobStream.ts line 101): uses `/api/v1/jobs/${targetJobId}/stream`
- Check the old UI (_app-reference/src/lib/api/streaming.ts) and the API routes (agent-api/src/routes/) to verify the correct endpoint path

### 3. Chat panel - add essential assistant messages from stream
Check how the old UI adds "essential" messages to the chat during streaming. Look at:
- `_app-reference/src/hooks/useJobStream.ts` or `_app-reference/src/hooks/useAgentExecution.ts`
- See if there are essential status messages added during phases (e.g., "Planning...", "Coding...", "Testing...")
- The new UI should also add these as assistant messages with `isEssential: true` so they show in the chat panel

### 4. Output panel improvements
The output panel already has good color coding. Review:
- `ekoa/components/builder/output-panel.tsx` - verify the color scheme is clear and distinct
- Add "reasoning" type handling if the old UI has it (check for thinking/reasoning events)
- Make sure tool_result content truncation is reasonable

### 5. Compare API client functions
Compare `ekoa/lib/api/client.ts` with `_app-reference/src/lib/api/client.ts`:
- Make sure `executeAgent()` sends the right request format
- Verify `startPreview()`, `stopPreview()`, `getPreviewProxyUrl()` functions are correct
- Check the stream URL endpoint

### 6. Verify the new UI stores match what the API expects
Compare the orchestration stores between old and new UI and make sure session/job management is compatible.

## Important Rules
- Do NOT modify files in _app-reference/ - it's read-only reference
- Only modify files in ekoa/
- Keep changes minimal and focused
- No emoji in UI code
- Test your changes compile (check for TypeScript errors)

## Files to Read First
1. `_app-reference/src/hooks/useAgentExecution.ts` - old execution hook
2. `_app-reference/src/hooks/useJobStream.ts` - old streaming hook  
3. `_app-reference/src/lib/api/streaming.ts` - old streaming lib
4. `_app-reference/src/lib/api/client.ts` - old API client (search for executeAgent, startPreview)
5. `agent-api/src/routes/index.ts` - API route registration to verify stream endpoint
6. `ekoa/hooks/useAgentExecution.ts` - new execution hook (FIX THIS)
7. `ekoa/hooks/useJobStream.ts` - new streaming hook (VERIFY/FIX)
8. `ekoa/lib/api/client.ts` - new API client (VERIFY/FIX)
9. `ekoa/components/builder/output-panel.tsx` - output display (REVIEW/IMPROVE)
